story.title: The Princess and the Pea
story.logline: A prince seeks a real princess, and an old queen tests a storm-soaked visitor with a pea beneath many mattresses.

narratives.title: The Princess and the Pea MVP sample

perspectives.summary: The source scene follows the shared question of whether the visitor is a real princess.
perspectives.storytelling: The prince searches, the visitor arrives in a storm, the old queen arranges a test, and the household interprets the result.

players.name: prince
players.summary: A prince who wants to marry a real princess.
players.bio: The source says he travels all over the world to find a real princess and comes home sad.
players.storytelling: He marries the princess after the pea test identifies her as real.

players.name: princess
players.visual: rain-soaked from hair and clothes to shoes
players.summary: A visitor who arrives at the city gate during a terrible storm and says she is a real princess.
players.bio: The source gives no backstory beyond her arrival and claim.
players.storytelling: She sleeps on the prepared bed and reports feeling something hard through the bedding.

players.name: old queen
players.summary: The old queen who tests whether the visitor is a real princess.
players.bio: The source identifies her as the old queen.
players.storytelling: She places a pea beneath twenty mattresses and twenty eider-down beds.

players.name: old king
players.summary: The old king who opens the city gate during the storm.
players.bio: The source identifies him as the old king.
players.storytelling: He opens the gate after a knocking is heard.

storypoints.illustration: The household needs to determine whether the storm-soaked visitor is a real princess.
storypoints.summary: The shared source-level problem is testing the visitor's claim.
storypoints.storytelling: The old queen creates the pea test and the result leads the household to accept the visitor as a real princess.

overviews.label: Logline
overviews.summary: A prince seeks a real princess, and an old queen tests a storm-soaked visitor with a pea beneath many mattresses.
overviews.storytelling: Public-domain source-derived fixture context, not generated story prose.

overviews.label: Blended Throughlines
overviews.summary: Objective Story source-level context is present; Main Character, Influence Character, Relationship Story, CIPS, and dynamics remain unresolved.
overviews.storytelling: The fixture is intentionally conservative so missing evidence can be reported safely.
